pi_items.source: Calculated 38.9215 N degrees to radians (38.9215 * pi / 180 = 0.679308)
Calculated 77.0669 W degrees to radians (-77.0669 * pi / 180 = -1.345071)
Calculated using PyEphem/AstroPy for US Naval Observatory on 1876-07-04 UTC; actual transit is ~04:50 UTC
Calculated transit time ~04:50 UTC falls within this exact minute range
Plausible alternative hour fallback depending on Stellarium's exact Delta T / ephemeris variations
Plausible alternative minute fallback depending on Stellarium's exact Delta T / ephemeris variations

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real astronomical data and mathematical models simulating the real sky. Independent verification using PyEphem and AstroPy confirmed the target latitude (38.9215 N) and longitude (77.0669 W) perfectly convert to 0.6793 and -1.3450 radians respectively. The UTC transit time for the star Vega from the US Naval Observatory on July 4, 1876 was independently calculated to be approximately 04:50 UTC, satisfying the expected hour (4) and falling cleanly within the minute range [45, 59]. Expected file paths for screenshots and text logs were matched perfectly against export_result.sh.
